mnemonics: Maximum entropy is the maximum of the opponent's minimum
My opponent's strategy is my equilibrium
keyPoints: Mixed strategy Nash equilibrium occurs when no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming the other players keep their strategies unchanged.
The mixed strategy Nash equilibrium is a stable state where no player has an incentive to deviate from their strategy, as any deviation would lead to a worse outcome.
In a single population, the mixed strategy Nash equilibrium is the most stable outcome, as it takes into account the uncertainty and randomness of the players' actions.
